name: navigate
description: Load the given URL in the active tab and wait for a page-load signal (Page.loadEventFired OR Page.frameStoppedLoading, whichever fires first). Falls back to document.readyState check before giving up. After load, injects the querySelector :first-child→:first-of-type shim into the page. If network logging is enabled, events fired during navigation are captured automatically.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait for load

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); web.close()"

name: click
description: Click the first element matching selector. Uses _get_coords_full() with a fallback selector chain: (1) exact selector, (2) ':first-child' replaced with ':first-of-type', (3) structural pseudo-classes stripped entirely. Scrolls into view before measuring. Raises WebToolError if no variant matches.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.click('selector'); web.close()"
fallback: Use coordinate_click when all selector variants fail

name: double_click
description: Double-click the first element matching selector. Sends two click pairs (clickCount=1 then clickCount=2) to satisfy browser dblclick detection. Raises WebToolError if no element found.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.double_click('#editable-cell'); web.close()"

name: triple_click
description: Triple-click the first element matching selector, which selects all text inside an input/textarea. More reliable than Ctrl+A on pages that intercept keyboard shortcuts. Raises WebToolError if no element found.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.triple_click('input#search'); web.close()"

name: type
description: Type text into the element matched by selector.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.fill('selector', 'text'); web.close()"

name: clear
description: Clear the value of an input or textarea matched by selector. Sets value to empty string and dispatches input/change events.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.clear('input#search'); web.close()"

name: screenshot
description: Take a JPEG screenshot and return the local file path (or raw base-64 if no filename given).
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.screenshot('screenshots/debug.jpg'); web.close()"
note: Use sparingly — only when DOM inspection is insufficient.

name: key
description: Press a keyboard key (Tab, Enter, Escape, ArrowDown, ArrowUp, etc.). Sends both keyDown and keyUp events.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.key('Tab'); web.close()"

name: execute_js
description: Evaluate JavaScript in the page context and return the result. Wrap objects/arrays in JSON.stringify(). Note: document.querySelector and document.querySelectorAll in the page have been shimmed to automatically retry ':first-child' selectors as ':first-of-type' when no element is found.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print(web.js('document.title')); web.close()"

name: wait
description: Poll for a CSS selector at 0.1 s intervals; returns True as soon as the element appears, False on timeout.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); found = web.wait('.my-class', 15); print(found); web.close()"

name: wait_for_visible
description: Poll at 0.1 s intervals until the element matching selector is both present in the DOM AND computed-visible (display != 'none', visibility != 'hidden', opacity != '0'). Returns True when visible, False on timeout.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.hover('.menu-item'); ok = web.wait_for_visible('.dropdown', 5); web.close()"

name: wait_for_text
description: Poll at 0.1 s intervals until the element matching selector contains the given text in its textContent. Returns True when matched, False on timeout.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.click('button#submit'); ok = web.wait_for_text('#status', 'Success', 10); web.close()"

name: wait_for_navigation
description: Block until the next page-load event fires (Page.loadEventFired or Page.frameStoppedLoading). Call this immediately after clicking a link or submit button to wait for the resulting navigation. Re-injects the querySelector shim after navigation. All CDP events received while waiting are also routed through _dispatch_event so network logging continues seamlessly.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.click('a.nav-link'); web.wait_for_navigation(15); web.close()"

name: wait_for_url_change
description: Capture the current URL then poll at 0.1 s intervals until window.location.href changes. Returns the new URL on success, or the original URL if the timeout expires. Use immediately after click() on a navigation link/button to confirm the page changed before reading content.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('https://example.com'); web.click('a'); new_url = web.wait_for_url_change(10); print(new_url); web.close()"

name: scroll
description: Scroll the page in a given direction by a number of pixels. Useful for revealing lazy-loaded content.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); web.scroll('down', 800); web.close()"

name: hover
description: Move the mouse over the first element matching selector to trigger :hover effects and reveal dropdown menus. Two-layer approach: (1) Input.dispatchMouseEvent(mouseMoved); (2) CSS.forcePseudoState applied to the hovered node AND its ancestors (up to 5 levels). Ancestor hovering ensures CSS rules like '.figure:hover .figcaption { display:block }' activate when you hover a child element. Uses the fallback-chain: exact selector, then ':first-child'→':first-of-type', then stripped pseudo-classes.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.hover('nav .menu-item'); web.wait_for_visible('.dropdown-menu', 3); web.close()"

name: select_option
description: Set a <select> dropdown to the option with the given value attribute, then fire a change event.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.select_option('#country', 'US'); web.close()"

name: is_visible
description: Return True if the element matching selector is present in the DOM AND computed-visible (display != 'none', visibility != 'hidden', opacity != '0').
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print(web.is_visible('.modal')); web.close()"

name: get_computed_style
description: Return the computed CSS property value for the element matching selector. Returns None if the element is not found.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.hover('.figure'); print(web.get_computed_style('.figcaption', 'display')); web.close()"

name: get_element_count
description: Return the number of DOM elements matching selector. Single JS round-trip. Returns 0 if none match.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print(web.get_element_count('.quote')); web.close()"

name: find_by_text
description: Find the first element whose visible text exactly matches the given string. Returns {tagName, selector} or null.

tag.description: Optional tag name to narrow the search (e.g. 'button', 'a', 'li')

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); info = web.find_by_text('Sign in', 'button'); print(info); web.close()"

name: click_by_text
description: Find the first element with the given visible text and click it. Raises WebToolError if not found.

tag.description: Optional tag name to narrow the search

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.click_by_text('Submit', 'button'); web.close()"

name: get_all_links
description: Return a list of all non-javascript: href URLs found on the current page. Single batched JS call.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); links = web.get_all_links(); print(json.dumps(links, indent=2)); web.close()"

name: get_table
description: Extract the first <table> matching selector as a list of dicts. Header cells become dict keys; each body row becomes one dict. Raises WebToolError if no table found.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); rows = web.get_table('#myTable'); print(json.dumps(rows, indent=2)); web.close()"

name: get_form_fields
description: Introspect form structure. Returns a list of dicts (one per input/select/textarea) with keys: tag, type, name, id, value, placeholder, required, options (for select elements — list of {value, text}).

selector.description: CSS selector for the form element

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); fields = web.get_form_fields('#login-form'); print(json.dumps(fields, indent=2)); web.close()"

name: get_page_structure
description: Return a structural summary of the current page in a single Runtime.evaluate round-trip. Result dict keys: title, url, h1s, h2s, link_count, form_count, table_count.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); print(json.dumps(web.get_page_structure(), indent=2)); web.close()"

name: upload_file
description: Set a file-input element's files via DOM.setFileInputFiles — no OS file-picker dialog appears. Raises WebToolError if element is not found or not a file input.

selector.description: CSS selector for the <input type='file'> element

file_path.description: Absolute path to the file to upload

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.upload_file('input[type=file]', '/tmp/doc.pdf'); web.close()"

name: login
description: All-in-one login helper. Navigates to url, fills username and password fields, clicks submit, waits up to timeout seconds for the URL to change, and returns True if a redirect occurred (indicating successful login). Eliminates the navigate→fill→fill→click→wait pattern for login tasks.

username_selector.description: CSS selector for the username/email input

password_selector.description: CSS selector for the password input

submit_selector.description: CSS selector for the submit button

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait for post-login redirect

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); ok = web.login('https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/login', '#username', 'tomsmith', '#password', 'SuperSecretPassword!', \"button[type='submit']\"); print('logged in:', ok, web.js('window.location.href')); web.close()"

name: fill_form
description: Fill multiple form fields in one call. Accepts a dict mapping CSS selector to value. Detects field type automatically: <select> uses select_option(), checkbox/radio uses click() (toggled based on truthy/falsy value), all other inputs use fill(). Raises WebToolError if any selector is not found.

field_map.description: Dict mapping CSS selector strings to value strings

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.go('URL'); web.fill_form({'#first': 'Alice', '#last': 'Smith', '#email': 'alice@example.com'}); web.click('button[type=submit]'); web.close()"

name: paginate
description: Collect data across multiple pages automatically. On each page: calls extractor_fn(web) to collect a list of items, then clicks next_selector if present and waits for navigation. Stops when next_selector disappears or max_pages is reached. extractor_fn must accept a WebTool and return a list (or JSON string of a list). Returns a flat list of all collected items.

next_selector.description: CSS selector for the 'next page' link or button

implementation: results = web.paginate('li.next a', lambda w: json.loads(w.js("JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.quote .text')).map(e=>e.textContent.trim()))")), max_pages=5)
bash_usage:
python3 -c "
import json
from tools.web_tool import WebTool
web = WebTool(port=9222)
web.connect()
web.go('https://quotes.toscrape.com')
results = web.paginate(
    'li.next a',
    lambda w: json.loads(w.js(\"JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.quote .text')).map(e=>e.textContent.trim()))\")),
    max_pages=3
)
print(len(results), 'quotes')
web.close()
"

name: open_tab
description: Open a new browser tab and return its tab id string. Does NOT switch focus — call switch_tab(tab_id) to interact with the new tab.

url.description: Initial URL for the new tab

bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); tid = web.open_tab('https://example.com'); web.switch_tab(tid); print(web.js('document.title')); web.close()"

name: switch_tab
description: Reconnect the WebSocket to the tab identified by tab_id. All subsequent commands operate on that tab. Re-injects the querySelector shim after switching. If network logging was active, Network.enable is re-sent to the new tab connection.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); tid = web.open_tab('https://example.com'); web.switch_tab(tid); web.close()"

name: close_tab
description: Close the tab identified by tab_id. If it is the current tab, the WebSocket is also closed.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); tid = web.open_tab(); web.close_tab(tid); web.close()"

name: get_open_tabs
description: Return a list of dicts for every open page-type tab, each with keys: id, url, title.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print(json.dumps(web.get_open_tabs(), indent=2)); web.close()"

name: enable_network_logging
description: Enable CDP Network domain and start collecting all network events (requests, responses, failures) into an in-process log. Call this BEFORE navigating so events during page load are captured. Resets the log on each call. All subsequent cmd()/go()/wait_* calls automatically route Network.* events to the log without additional user code.
bash_usage:
python3 -c "
from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json
web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect()
web.enable_network_logging()
web.go('https://quotes.toscrape.com')
log = web.get_network_log()
print(f'Captured {len(log)} events')
print(json.dumps([e for e in log if e[\"type\"] == \"Network.responseReceived\"][:3], indent=2))
web.close()
"
note: Must be called before navigation to capture page-load requests. Log persists across navigations until enable_network_logging() is called again.

name: get_network_log
description: Return all network events collected since the last enable_network_logging() call as a list of dicts. Each entry has at minimum: type, timestamp, requestId, url. Requests also have: method, resourceType. Responses also have: status, mimeType, resourceType. loadingFinished entries have encodedDataLength. loadingFailed entries have errorText.
bash_usage:
python3 -c "
from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json
web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect()
web.enable_network_logging()
web.go('https://quotes.toscrape.com')
log = web.get_network_log()
xhr = [e for e in log if e['type'] == 'Network.responseReceived']
print(json.dumps(xhr[:5], indent=2))
web.close()
"

entry_types.Network.responseReceived: url, status, mimeType, resourceType, requestId, timestamp

name: wait_for_request
description: Block until a network request whose URL contains the given pattern fires. Checks the already-collected log first (returns immediately if already seen), then polls the WebSocket for new events. enable_network_logging() is called automatically if not yet enabled. Returns a dict with keys: type, url, method, resourceType, requestId, timestamp — or None on timeout. Ideal for confirming that a SPA triggered an API call after a user action.

pattern.description: Substring to match against the full request URL (e.g. '/api/search', '.json')

bash_usage:
python3 -c "
from tools.web_tool import WebTool
web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect()
web.enable_network_logging()
web.go('https://spa.example.com')
web.click('#load-data')
req = web.wait_for_request('/api/items', timeout=10)
if req:
    print('API called:', req['url'])
web.close()
"
note: Call enable_network_logging() before navigation to avoid missing early requests.

name: wait_for_response
description: Block until a complete network response (headers + body loaded) whose URL contains the given pattern is available. Waits for Network.responseReceived then Network.loadingFinished for the same requestId, then fetches up to 500 chars of the response body via Network.getResponseBody. Returns {url, status, mimeType, resourceType, requestId, body_preview} or None on timeout. enable_network_logging() is called automatically if not yet enabled.

pattern.description: Substring to match against the full response URL (e.g. '/api/search', '.json')

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait for the complete response

bash_usage:
python3 -c "
from tools.web_tool import WebTool
web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect()
web.enable_network_logging()
web.go('https://spa.example.com')
web.click('#load-data')
resp = web.wait_for_response('/api/items', timeout=10)
if resp:
    print(resp['status'], resp['body_preview'][:200])
web.close()
"

return_fields.resourceType: CDP resource type (XHR, Fetch, Document, etc.)
return_fields.requestId: CDP request ID for further lookups
return_fields.body_preview: First 500 characters of the decoded response body (empty string if unavailable)

note: body_preview is populated only for text responses where Network.getResponseBody succeeds. Binary/encoded responses may have an empty body_preview.

name: coordinate_click
description: Click at specific x,y page coordinates. Fallback when CSS selectors cannot identify the target.
implementation: web.cmd('Input.dispatchMouseEvent', {'type': 'mousePressed', 'x': x, 'y': y, 'button': 'left', 'clickCount': 1}); web.cmd('Input.dispatchMouseEvent', {'type': 'mouseReleased', 'x': x, 'y': y, 'button': 'left', 'clickCount': 1})
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); web.cmd('Input.dispatchMouseEvent', {'type': 'mousePressed', 'x': 400, 'y': 200, 'button': 'left', 'clickCount': 1}); web.cmd('Input.dispatchMouseEvent', {'type': 'mouseReleased', 'x': 400, 'y': 200, 'button': 'left', 'clickCount': 1}); web.close()"
when_to_use: When CSS selectors fail or JavaScript execution fails.

name: get_page_info
description: Return basic page metadata: URL, title, input count, button count.
implementation: web.js('JSON.stringify({url: window.location.href, title: document.title, inputCount: document.querySelectorAll("input").length, buttonCount: document.querySelectorAll("button").length})')
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; import json; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); print(json.loads(web.js('JSON.stringify({url: window.location.href, title: document.title})'))); web.close()"

name: verify_completion
description: Check if task completed successfully by inspecting URL change and presence of success keywords in page text.
implementation: Compare web.js('window.location.href') with initial_url; search web.js('document.body.textContent.toLowerCase()') for success_keywords.
bash_usage: python3 -c "from tools.web_tool import WebTool; web = WebTool(port=9222); web.connect(); final_url = web.js('window.location.href'); page_text = web.js('document.body.textContent.toLowerCase()'); success = any(k in page_text for k in ['success','complete','confirmed']); print(f'URL changed: {final_url != initial_url}, Success: {success}'); web.close()"

name: browser_pool
description: Create and manage an async pool of N independent headless Chrome processes. Supports 'async with' context manager and pool.map() for parallel task dispatch. Each slot owns a dedicated AsyncWebTool. AsyncWebTool gains enable_network_logging_async, get_network_log_async, wait_for_request_async, wait_for_response_async for SPA network inspection in async tasks.

size.description: Number of Chrome processes in the pool

implementation:
from tools.web_tool import BrowserPool

async with BrowserPool(size=2) as pool:
    results = await pool.map(async_fn, [url1, url2])
bash_usage:
python3 -c "
import asyncio
from tools.web_tool import BrowserPool, AsyncWebTool

async def fetch(web, url):
    await web.go_async(url)
    return await web.js_async('document.title')

async def main():
    async with BrowserPool(size=2) as pool:
        titles = await pool.map(fetch, ['https://example.com', 'https://quotes.toscrape.com'])
    print(titles)

asyncio.run(main())
"
map_signature: async def fn(web: AsyncWebTool, arg: Any) -> Any

name: async_web_tool
description: Fully async CDP browser automation via AsyncWebTool. Gen 7 adds: enable_network_logging_async(), get_network_log_async(), wait_for_request_async(pattern, timeout), wait_for_response_async(pattern, timeout). Network events are dispatched in _recv_loop so they are captured even when no command is in flight. hover_async() forces :hover on hovered node AND ancestors. querySelector shim injected on connect and after each navigation.
implementation:
from tools.web_tool import AsyncWebTool
aweb = AsyncWebTool(port=9222)
await aweb.connect()
await aweb.enable_network_logging_async()
await aweb.go_async('https://spa.example.com')
await aweb.click_async('#load')
resp = await aweb.wait_for_response_async('/api/data', timeout=10)
print(resp)
await aweb.close()
bash_usage:
python3 -c "import asyncio; from tools.web_tool import AsyncWebTool
async def main():
    web = AsyncWebTool(9222)
    await web.connect()
    await web.go_async('https://example.com')
    print(await web.js_async('document.title'))
    await web.close()
asyncio.run(main())"

name: clone_website
description: Create a pixel-perfect website replica with all assets.
implementation: Follow clone/CLONING_PROTOCOL.md for systematic high-fidelity cloning.
bash_usage: Use WebTool + WebFetch + Write following the complete protocol.
note: Perfect replica cloning — not just content extraction.
